Subscribers
padenot

Details

Reviewers
padenot
Group Reviewers
media-playback-reviewers
Bugzilla Bug ID
2034990
Summary

Introduce a process-wide singleton that wraps a PDMFactory and exposes
only the read-only support-query API (IsTypeSupported, IsSupported). The
singleton is built lazily and rebuilt when prefs or gfxVars relevant to
PDM enumeration change, eliminating the per-call PDMFactory
construction cost while keeping codec-support answers correct after a
pref toggle or GPU-process restart.

CreateDecoder and SetCDMProxy continue to use new PDMFactory() because
they depend on per-stream state. The wrapper does not expose them, so
the type system enforces the split.

Diff Detail

rFIREFOXAUTOLAND firefox-autoland

Comment Actions

Some simplification about "only runs once" invariants but it should work as is.

dom/media/platforms/PDMFactorySupport.cpp
116

std::once?

159

std::once?

This revision is now accepted and ready to land.Mon, May 11, 3:06 PM2026-05-11 15:06:18 (UTC+0)
padenot added a comment.Mon, May 11, 3:09 PM2026-05-11 15:09:02 (UTC+0)
Comment Actions

Did you investigate doing everything throught the singleton?

PDMSupport::IsTypeSupported synchronously returns the information without getting the instance, then querying? Was there issues with that?

alwu added a comment.Mon, May 11, 6:38 PM2026-05-11 18:38:52 (UTC+0)
Comment Actions
In D299302#10394054, @padenot wrote:

Did you investigate doing everything throught the singleton?

PDMSupport::IsTypeSupported synchronously returns the information without getting the instance, then querying? Was there issues with that?

The testing so far looks good. Using IsTypeSupported is a good idea, will try. If query passes shutdown, then we just return not supported, it shouldn't cause any issue. Will do another round of testing.
